explanations.1: This special line tells the system to use whatever bash interpreter is available in the environment, rather than assuming it's in a fixed location like /bin/bash.
explanations.36: The 'source' command reads and executes another shell script file (log.sh) inside the current script, making any functions from that file available to use here.
explanations.53: $* expands to all the command-line arguments passed to this script; we're passing them along to the __main function so it can use them.
